content.full_text:
Norm Fuller                                          July 23, 1976
Franchise Tax Board


Rich Eichman

Lobbyist Reporting


Recently, you presented the following statements to the Commission:

     1. According to Section 86107(f), the agencies lobbied must be
        reported.

     2. The definition of payment for credit card accounts is at the
        time the charge is made. Therefore, the incurrence and pay-
        ment of the expense are at the same date and the filer has
        no choice as to when to report it.

The staff has discussed the above statements and reached the follow-
ing conclusions with respect to each:

     1. A lobbyist must disclose an agency in "Agency Involved", Form
        620 or 625, only when the lobbyist has attempted to influence
        the administrative action of that agency. If the lobbyist is
        attempting to influence a bill before the legislature the
        lobbyist need only disclose the "Legislature" as the agency
        involved.

     2. When a lobbyist presents a credit card for payment of a general
        lobbying expense the lobbyist has merely incurred an expense.
        Payment of the expense is not made until payment is made to the
        credit company. Accordingly a lobbyist may disclose general
        lobbying expense on the report when payment is made to the
        credit company.

If you have any questions regarding the above advice please contact me.


bcc: Jeanne P.      Gina
     Dan            Mike
     Ken F.         Ed
     Ted            Bob S.
     Natalie
     Nan


Given the poor legibility of this pink document, I cannot provide an accurate verbatim transcription without inserting numerous [illegible] markers throughout, which would not be useful. The document appears to be several paragraphs of typewritten text with what seems to be a signature block at the bottom, but the specific words cannot be reliably read from this image.
